sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः । शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कुन्तीनन्दन  यह पुरुष स्वयं अनादि और गुणोंसे रहित होनेसे अविनाशी परमात्मस्वरूप ही है ।  यह शरीरमें रहता हुआ भी न करता है और न लिप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: O son of Kunti, this supreme self is beginningless, beyond the gunas, and imperishable. Though abiding in the body, it does not act and is not stained.
essence: What you are remains unstained, even while life moves through you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The true self has no beginning, is beyond the three qualities of nature, and cannot be destroyed. Even while living in a body, it neither performs actions nor gets tainted by them.
simpleInsight: You can be fully present in life without being trapped by everything that happens through you.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before battle. Krishna now explains the inner reality behind action: the true self is beginningless, beyond the changing qualities of nature, and remains untouched even while embodied. This prepares Arjuna to act without confusion.
modernRelevance: You said something sharp in anger, then spent the whole night replaying it. The mind wants to fuse you with the mistake; this verse separates the actor from the deeper witness.
translationLiteral: अनादित्वात् (because of beginninglessness) / निर्गुणत्वात् (because of being beyond the gunas) / परमात्मा (supreme self) / अयम् (this) / अव्ययः (imperishable) । / शरीरस्थः (abiding in the body) / अपि (even though) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / न (not) / करोति (acts) / न (not) / लिप्यते (is stained)
insight: It shifts identity away from what the body and mind do toward the untouched awareness that remains.

quoteVariants.text: The supreme self is untouched. It lives in the body, yet never acts or stains.
I live in the body, but I am not the doer.
